cards.en: We might/may not be going anywhere this year.
cards.cz: Možná, že letos nikam nepojedeme.

cards.en: We might/may be going to Turkey this year.
cards.cz: Možná, že letos pojedeme do Turecka.

cards.en: They might/may prefer red.
cards.cz: Možná, že mají raději červené.

cards.en: She might have been waving at us.
cards.cz: Možná, že mávala na nás.

cards.en: He might/may not want to see me.
cards.cz: Možná, že mě nechce vidět, [on]

cards.en: He might have been right.
cards.cz: Možná, že měl pravdu.

cards.en: They might have been speaking about something quite different.
cards.cz: Možná, že mluvili o něčem docela jiném.

cards.en: My father might/may be able to suggest something.
cards.cz: Možná, že můj otec bude moct něco navrhnout.

cards.en: She might have forgotten about us.
cards.cz: Možná, že na nás zapomněla.

cards.en: Jim might/may be able to tell us something about it.
cards.cz: Možná, že nám o tom Jim bude moct něco říct.

cards.en: He might/may be looking for us.
cards.cz: Možná, že nás hledá, [on]

cards.en: He might/may not be expecting us.
cards.cz: Možná, že nás neočekává, [on]

cards.en: There might/may not be enough time.
cards.cz: Možná, že nebude dost času.

cards.en: She might/may not be able to walk for a few months.
cards.cz: Možná, že nebude moct několik měsíců chodit, lona]

cards.en: He might/may not have to buy a ticket.
cards.cz: Možná, že nebude muset koupit lístek, [on]

cards.en: You might/may not have to sign anything.
cards.cz: Možná, že nebudeš muset nic podepsat.

cards.en: They might/may not have (the) time.
cards.cz: Možná, že nebudou mít čas.

cards.en: They might/may not be hungry.
cards.cz: Možná, že nebudou mít hlad.

cards.en: I might/may not have to leave tomorrow.
cards.cz: Možná, že nebudu muset zítra odjet.

cards.en: They might not have been looking for you.
cards.cz: Možná, že nehledali tebe.

cards.en: She might/may not be interested.
cards.cz: Možná, že nemá zájem, [ona]

cards.en: We might/may not be speaking/talking about the same person.
cards.cz: Možná, že nemluvíme o tom samém člověku.

cards.en: He might not have understood my question.
cards.cz: Možná, že nerozuměl mé otázce.

cards.en: He might not have been telling the truth.
cards.cz: Možná, že neříkal pravdu.

cards.en: They might/may not know about it.
cards.cz: Možná, že o tom nevědí.

cards.en: She might/may need our help.
cards.cz: Možná, že potřebuje naši pomoc, [ona]

cards.en: He might/may not agree with us.
cards.cz: Možná, že s námi nebude souhlasit, [on]

cards.en: He might/may bring two friends with him.
cards.cz: Možná, že s sebou přivede dva kamarády, [on]

cards.en: They might have got lost on the way here.
cards.cz: Možná, že se cestou sem ztratili.

cards.en: They might/may not like his music.
cards.cz: Možná, že se jim jeho hudba nelíbí.

cards.en: She might/may be looking forward to it.
cards.cz: Možná, že se na to těší, [ona]

cards.en: The weather might/may change suddenly/might/may suddenly change.
cards.cz: Možná, že se najednou změní počasí.

cards.en: James might have been trying to call us while/when we were away.
cards.cz: Možná, že se nám James pokoušel zavolat, když jsme byli pryč.

cards.en: They might/may be trying to contact us.
cards.cz: Možná, že se nás pokoušejí zkontaktovat.

cards.en: She might not have been feeling well.
cards.cz: Možná, že se necítila dobře.

cards.en: He might/may not be looking at us.
cards.cz: Možná, že se nedívá na nás. [on]

cards.en: She might/may not be laughing at you.
cards.cz: Možná, že se nesměje tobě, [ona]

cards.en: I might/may be studying/learning French from September.
cards.cz: Možná, že se od září budu učit francouzský.

cards.en: They might/may get married next summer.
cards.cz: Možná, že se příští léto vezmou.

cards.en: She might have decided against it.
cards.cz: Možná, že se rozhodla [proti tomu] být proti.

cards.en: You might/may not be able to contact him this week.
cards.cz: Možná, že se s ním tento týden nebudeš moct spojit.

cards.en: They might have been hiding in the cellar.
cards.cz: Možná, že se schovávali ve sklepě.

cards.en: We might/may not be able to get there by eight.
cards.cz: Možná, že se tam do osmi nebudeme moct dostat.

cards.en: The concert might/may not take place.
cards.cz: Možná, že se ten koncert neuskuteční.

cards.en: We might/may never see each other again.
cards.cz: Možná, že se už nikdy znovu neuvidíme.

cards.en: She might/may think it's a good idea.
cards.cz: Možná, že si bude myslet, že je to dobrý nápad, [ona]

cards.en: She might/may not remember us.
cards.cz: Možná, že si nás nebude pamatovat, [ona]

cards.en: She might not have been complaining about that.
cards.cz: Možná, že si nestěžovala na tohle.

cards.en: I might/may be able to take a few days of holiday/a few days' holiday next month.
cards.cz: Možná, že si příští měsíc budu moct vzít pár dní dovolené.

cards.en: We might/may have to make a reservation.
cards.cz: Možná, že si to budeme muset zamluvit [budeme muset udělat rezervaci].

cards.en: I might/may buy a new camera on Saturday.
cards.cz: Možná, že si v sobotu koupím nový foťák.

cards.en: She might not have recognized you.
cards.cz: Možná, že tě nepoznala.

cards.en: I might/may have to write the letter again.
cards.cz: Možná, že ten dopis budu muset napsat znovu.

cards.en: We might/may not be able to find the house in the dark.
cards.cz: Možná, že ten dům nebudeme moct ve tmě najít.

cards.en: The film might/may be interesting.
cards.cz: Možná, že ten film bude zajímavý.

cards.en: He might/may not believe you.
cards.cz: Možná, že ti nebude věřit, [on]

cards.en: I might/may not be able to let you know until Thursday.
cards.cz: Možná, že ti nebudu moct dát vědět až do čtvrtka.

cards.en: She might/may not understand you.
cards.cz: Možná, že ti nerozumí, [ona]

cards.en: You might/may be able to buy it more cheaply elsewhere/somewhere else.
cards.cz: Možná, že to budeš moct koupit někde jinde levněji.

cards.en: It might/may not be enough.
cards.cz: Možná, že to nebude dost.

cards.en: I might/may send it registered.
cards.cz: Možná, že to pošlu doporučeně.

cards.en: She might/may pass the exam, but she might/may not.
cards.cz: Možná, že tu zkoušku udělá, ale možná, že ne. [ona]

cards.en: They might/may not be staying with us for more than a few nights.
cards.cz: Možná, že u nás nezůstanou víc než pár nocí.

cards.en: They might/may not be going out together/going out with each other any more.
cards.cz: Možná, že už spolu nechodí.

cards.en: We might/may not have to pay for it.
cards.cz: Možná, že za to nebudeme muset zaplatit.

cards.en: I might/may be seeing Elizabeth tomorrow afternoon.
cards.cz: Možná, že zítra odpoledne uvidím Elizabeth.

cards.en: My passport isn't here. I had it this morning. Someone must have stolen it.
cards.cz: Můj pas tady není. Dnes ráno jsem ho měl. Někdo ho musel ukrást.

cards.en: My finger wouldn't stop bleeding.
cards.cz: Můj prst nepřestával [nechtěl přestat] krvácet.

cards.en: My older brother had to study Latin at school.
cards.cz: Můj starší bratr se ve škole musel učit latinu.

cards.en: I had to borrow (the/some) money from a/my friend.
cards.cz: Musel jsem si půjčit peníze od přítele.

cards.en: Have you ever had to complain in a restaurant?
cards.cz: Musel sis někdy stěžovat v restauraci?

cards.en: We had to wait (for) forty-five minutes.
cards.cz: Museli jsme čekat čtyřicet pět minut.

cards.en: We had to take a taxi.
cards.cz: Museli jsme si vzít taxi.

cards.en: We had to start/begin again.
cards.cz: Museli jsme začít znovu.

cards.en: They had to call a/the doctor.
cards.cz: Museli zavolat lékaře.

cards.en: It must be beautiful to live on a boat.
cards.cz: Musí být krásné bydlet na lodi.

cards.en: He's had to wear glasses since he was five.
cards.cz: Musí nosit brýle od pěti let [od té doby, co mu bylo pět].

cards.en: They must think we don't want to see them any more.
cards.cz: Musí si myslet, že je už nechceme vidět, [oni]

cards.en: Do children have to wear/Have children got to wear school uniform in your country?
cards.cz: Musí ve vaší zemi děti nosit školní uniformu?

cards.en: I must leave/I have to leave/I've got to leave at half past nine.
cards.cz: Musím odejít o půl desáté.

cards.en: I must tell/I have to tell/I've got to tell you something important.
cards.cz: Musím vám říct něco důležitého.

cards.en: We must be/We have to be/We've got to be there.
cards.cz: Musíme tam být.

cards.en: Do you have to have/Have you got to have a visa for Australia?
cards.cz: Musíš mít do Austrálie vízum?

cards.en: You must be very proud of her.
cards.cz: Musíš na ni být velmi hrdá.

cards.en: You must ask/You have to ask/You've got to ask the secretary.
cards.cz: Musíš se zeptat sekretářky.

cards.en: Can she take us to the airport?
cards.cz: Může nás vzít na letiště? [ona]

cards.en: Can he come too?
cards.cz: Může přijít také? [on]

cards.en: We can come at seven.
cards.cz: Můžeme přijít v sedm.

cards.en: Can we smoke here?
cards.cz: Můžeme tady kouřit?

cards.en: We can do it together.
cards.cz: Můžeme to udělat společně.

cards.en: You can use my dictionary.
cards.cz: Můžeš použít můj slovník.

cards.en: Can you translate this for me?
cards.cz: Můžeš pro mě tohle přeložit?

cards.en: You can ask my father.
cards.cz: Můžeš se zeptat mého otce.

cards.en: Can you give me his telephone number?
cards.cz: Můžete mi dát jeho telefonní číslo?

cards.en: I thought you could swim!
cards.cz: Myslel jsem si, že umíš plavat!

cards.en: I think it's going to rain. You'd better borrow my umbrella.
cards.cz: Myslím, že bude pršet. Raději by sis měl půjčit můj deštník.

cards.en: Do you think I should invite him?
cards.cz: Myslíš, že bych ho měl pozvat?

cards.en: Do you think we'd better warn her?
cards.cz: Myslíš, že bychom ji raději měli varovat?

cards.en: Do you think we should help?
cards.cz: Myslíš, že bychom měli pomoct?

cards.en: Do you think he could have won the competition?
cards.cz: Myslíš, že tu soutěž mohl vyhrát?

cards.en: Do you think your brother will be able to advise/your brother is going to be able to advise me?
cards.cz: Myslíte, že mi váš bratr bude moct poradit?

cards.en: Shall we visit the castle tomorrow?
cards.cz: Navštívíme zítra ten hrad?

cards.en: He won't be allowed to see/He's not going to be allowed to see her any more.
cards.cz: Nebude ji už smět vídat, [on]

cards.en: She won't be allowed to go/She's not going to be allowed to go with us. She's only fourteen.
cards.cz: Nebude s námi smět jít. Je jí teprve čtrnáct.

cards.en: We won't have to wait long.
cards.cz: Nebudeme muset dlouho čekat.

cards.en: We won't have to pay immediately.
cards.cz: Nebudeme muset platit okamžitě.

cards.en: You won't have to go far.
cards.cz: Nebudeš muset jít daleko.

cards.en: You won't have to cook for us. We'll go out.
cards.cz: Nebudeš pro nás muset vařit. Půjdeme ven.

cards.en: You won't be able to return/You're not going to be able to return the books this afternoon. The library's closed today.
cards.cz: Nebudeš ty knihy moct vrátit dnes odpoledne. Knihovna je dnes zavřená.

cards.en: You won't have to say anything.
cards.cz: Nebudete muset nic říkat.

cards.en: It wasn't bad, but it could have been better.
cards.cz: Nebylo to špatné, ale mohlo to být lepší.

cards.en: Something's burning. Can you smell it?
cards.cz: Něco se pálí. Cítíš to?

cards.en: We couldn't find your house on the map.
cards.cz: Nedokázali jsme na mapě najít tvůj dům.

cards.en: My pen won't write.
cards.cz: Nechce mi psát pero.

cards.en: I've warned him several times but he won't listen.
cards.cz: Několikrát jsem ho varoval, ale on neuposlechl. [odmítá poslechnout]

cards.en: He shouldn't smoke so much.
cards.cz: Neměl by tolik kouřit.

cards.en: You shouldn't be so lazy.
cards.cz: Neměl bys být tak líný.

cards.en: Shouldn't you try to explain it to her?
cards.cz: Neměl bys jí to zkusit vysvětlit?

cards.en: You shouldn't spend so much money.
cards.cz: Neměl bys utrácet tolik peněz.

cards.en: He shouldn't have been so impatient.
cards.cz: Neměl být tak netrpělivý.

cards.en: I shouldn't have got up this morning!
cards.cz: Neměl jsem dnes ráno vstát!

cards.en: I shouldn't have trusted them.
cards.cz: Neměl jsem jim důvěřovat.

cards.en: You shouldn't have suggested it.
cards.cz: Neměl jsi to navrhovat.

cards.en: You shouldn't have drunk so much.
cards.cz: Neměl jsi tolik pít.

cards.en: He shouldn't have said that.
cards.cz: Neměl to říkat.

cards.en: She shouldn't be so impatient.
cards.cz: Neměla by být tak netrpělivá.

cards.en: She shouldn't have been eating.
cards.cz: Neměla jíst.

cards.en: She shouldn't have been talking.
cards.cz: Neměla mluvit.

cards.en: She shouldn't have been copying her friend.
cards.cz: Neměla opisovat od kamarádky.

cards.en: She shouldn't have been cheating.
cards.cz: Neměla podvádět.

cards.en: She shouldn't have been using a calculator.
cards.cz: Neměla používat kalkulačku.

cards.en: She shouldn't have gone there alone.
cards.cz: Neměla tam chodit sama.

cards.en: Shouldn't we ask at the information desk?
cards.cz: Neměli bychom se zeptat na informacích?

cards.en: Shouldn't we take it to the police?
cards.cz: Neměli bychom to vzít na policii?

cards.en: You shouldn't make mistakes with/in present tense.
cards.cz: Neměli byste dělat chyby v přítomném čase.

cards.en: We shouldn't have ignored his advice.
cards.cz: Neměli jsme ignorovat jeho radu.

cards.en: We shouldn't have promised (that) we'd come.
cards.cz: Neměli jsme slibovat, že přijdeme.

cards.en: We shouldn't have risked it.
cards.cz: Neměli jsme to riskovat.

cards.en: You can't have met her husband. She isn't married.
cards.cz: Nemohl jsi potkat jejího manžela. Není vdaná.

cards.en: You can't have phoned yesterday. I was at home all day and I didn't hear anything.
cards.cz: Nemohl jsi včera telefonovat. Byl jsem celý den doma a nic jsem neslyšel.

cards.en: He couldn't find the bathroom.
cards.cz: Nemohl najít koupelnu.

cards.en: He can't have liked our birthday present. I found it in the dustbin this morning.
cards.cz: Nemohl se mu líbit náš dárek k narozeninám. Dnes ráno jsem ho našla v koši.

cards.en: You can't/couldn't have washed very well. You were only in the bathroom (for) three minutes.
cards.cz: Nemohl ses moc dobře umýt. Byl jsi v koupelně jen tři minuty.

cards.en: Couldn't we find something cheaper?
cards.cz: Nemohli bychom najít něco levnějšího?

cards.en: We couldn't decide what to do.
cards.cz: Nemohli jsme se rozhodnout, co dělat.

cards.en: You can't/couldn't have done the journey in three hours. That's not possible/That's impossible.
cards.cz: Nemohli jste tu cestu urazit [udělat] za tři hodiny. To je nemožné.

cards.en: They couldn't believe my story.
cards.cz: Nemohli uvěřit mému příběhu.

cards.en: They can't believe it.
cards.cz: Nemohou tomu věřit.

cards.en: I can't find the/my keys. I must have left them at work.
cards.cz: Nemohu najít klíče. Musel jsem je nechat v práci.

cards.en: I can't answer these questions.
cards.cz: Nemohu odpovědět na tyto otázky.

cards.en: I can't remember his surname.
cards.cz: Nemohu si vzpomenout na jeho příjmení.

cards.en: I can't explain it.
cards.cz: Nemohu to vysvětlit.

cards.en: I didn't have to use a/the dictionary.
cards.cz: Nemusel jsem použít slovník.

cards.en: You needn't have bought so much. The fridge is already quite full.
cards.cz: Nemusel jsi (toho) tolik koupit. Lednička je už docela plná.

cards.en: You needn't have spoken so slowly. He speaks fluent Czech.
cards.cz: Nemusel jsi mluvit tak pomalu. On hovoří plynně česky.

cards.en: You needn't have sent it express. I won't need it until the end of next month.
cards.cz: Nemusel jsi to posílat expres. Budu to potřebovat až koncem příštího měsíce.

cards.en: You needn't have bought the book. They've got it in the library.
cards.cz: Nemusel jsi tu knihu kupovat. Mají ji v knihovně.

cards.en: He didn't have to buy a ticket.
cards.cz: Nemusel koupit lístek.

cards.en: She didn't have to help him with the letter.
cards.cz: Nemusela mu s tím dopisem pomoct.

cards.en: He doesn't have to wait/He hasn't got to wait/He needn't wait for us.
cards.cz: Nemusí na nás čekat, [on]

cards.en: She doesn't have to cook/She hasn't got to cook/She needn't cook for us.
cards.cz: Nemusí nám vařit, [ona]

cards.en: He doesn't have to come/He hasn't got to come/He needn't come.
cards.cz: Nemusí přijít, [on]

cards.en: They don't have to sit/They haven't got to sit/They needn't sit together.
cards.cz: Nemusí sedět spolu.

cards.en: It doesn't have to be/It hasn't got to be/It needn't be an expensive present.
cards.cz: Nemusí to být drahý dárek.

cards.en: We don't have to leave/We haven't got to leave/We needn't leave today.
cards.cz: Nemusíme odjet dnes.

cards.en: You don't have to bring/You haven't got to bring/You needn't bring anything.
cards.cz: Nemusíš nic nosit.

cards.en: You don't have to apologize/You haven't got to apologize/You needn't apologize.
cards.cz: Nemusíš se omlouvat.

cards.en: You don't have to ask/You haven't got to ask/You needn't ask.
cards.cz: Nemusíš se ptát.

cards.en: You don't have to explain/You haven't got to explain/You needn't explain (it).
cards.cz: Nemusíte to vysvětlovat.

cards.en: He can't find her.
cards.cz: Nemůže ji najít, [on]

cards.en: They can't like him (very) much. They never invite him to their parties.
cards.cz: Nemůže se jim moc líbit. Nikdy ho nezvou na své večírky.

cards.en: She can't be very interested in him. She never phones/calls or writes.
cards.cz: Nemůže se o něj moc zajímat. Nikdy nevolá ani nepíše, [ona]

cards.en: She can't remember.
cards.cz: Nemůže si vzpomenout, [ona]

cards.en: She can't do it.
cards.cz: Nemůže to udělat, [ona]

cards.en: You can't be hungry! We had lunch half an hour ago.
cards.cz: Nemůžeš mít hlad! Obědvali jsme před půl hodinou.

cards.en: He's not here. He must be at school.
cards.cz: Není tady. Musí být ve škole, [on]

cards.en: He wasn't allowed to go out because he had a lot of homework.
cards.cz: Nesměl jít ven, protože měl hodně úkolů.

cards.en: She wasn't allowed to go out with boys until she was nineteen.
cards.cz: Nesměla chodit s kluky, dokud jí nebylo devatenáct.

cards.en: It mustn't be too hot.
cards.cz: Nesmí být moc horko.

cards.en: She's not allowed to go/She can't go/She mustn't go out with boys.
cards.cz: Nesmí chodit s chlapci, [ona]

cards.en: He's not allowed to smoke/He can't smoke/He mustn't smoke in his room.
cards.cz: Nesmí kouřit ve svém pokoji, [on]

cards.en: He mustn't find us here.
cards.cz: Nesmí nás tady najít, [on]

cards.en: He's not allowed to bring/He can't bring/He mustn't bring friends home.
cards.cz: Nesmí si domů vodit kamarády, [on]

cards.en: She mustn't think we don't like it.
cards.cz: Nesmí si myslet, že se nám to nelíbí, [ona]

cards.en: They are not allowed to speak/They can't speak/They mustn't speak at the table.
cards.cz: Nesmí u stolu mluvit, [oni]

cards.en: She's not allowed to stay/She can't stay/She mustn't stay out after ten o'clock.
cards.cz: Nesmí zůstávat venku po desáté hodině, [ona]

cards.en: We mustn't forget to take our passports.
cards.cz: Nesmíme si zapomenout vzít pasy.

cards.en: You mustn't give a/the rabbit lettuce.
cards.cz: Nesmíš dávat králíkovi hlávkový salát.

cards.en: You mustn't leave the window open.
cards.cz: Nesmíš nechat otevřené okno.

cards.en: You mustn't break it.
cards.cz: Nesmíš to rozbít.

cards.en: He can't ski.
cards.cz: Neumí lyžovat, [on]

cards.en: She can't cook.
cards.cz: Neumí vařit, [ona]

cards.en: We can't sing.
cards.cz: Neumíme zpívat.

cards.en: I couldn't believe my eyes.
cards.cz: Nevěřil jsem svým očím.

cards.en: I don't believe you. It can't be that bad.
cards.cz: Nevěřím ti. Nemůže to být tak špatné.

cards.en: I've never been able to drink vodka.
cards.cz: Nikdy jsem nemohl pít vodku.

cards.en: They'll never/They won't ever be able to forgive him/They're never going to be able to forgive him.
cards.cz: Nikdy mu nebudou moct odpustit.

cards.en: He's never been allowed to drink alcohol at home.
cards.cz: Nikdy nesměl pít doma alkohol.

cards.en: She's never been allowed to go out with boys.
cards.cz: Nikdy nesměla chodit s kluky.

cards.en: They mustn't hear about this.
cards.cz: O tomhle nesmí slyšet, [oni]

cards.en: He's allowed to use his father's car at the weekends.
cards.cz: O víkendech smí používat tatínkovo auto, [on]

cards.en: I'm afraid I won't be able to come/I'm not going to be able to come to school tomorrow.
cards.cz: Obávám se, že zítra nebudu moct přijít do školy.

cards.en: Shall we order a/some dessert?
cards.cz: Objednáme si zákusek?

cards.en: Did you really knit that sweater yourself? It must have taken you a long time.
cards.cz: Opravdu jsi ten svetr upletla sama? Muselo ti to trvat dlouho.

cards.en: Really? That must have been terrible!
cards.cz: Opravdu? To muselo být strašné!

cards.en: Peter must work/Peter has to work/Peter's got to work on Saturday.
cards.cz: Peter musí v sobotu pracovat.

cards.en: Philip could have come too. Didn't you invite him?
cards.cz: Philip mohl přijít také. Ty jsi ho nepozval?

cards.en: Let's go now. It might/may be raining after lunch.
cards.cz: Pojďme teď. Možná, že po obědě bude pršet.

cards.en: I'd invite you to/for lunch if I could cook, but I can't.
cards.cz: Pozval bych tě na oběd, kdybych uměl vařit, ale neumím.

cards.en: Shall we invite Sally?
cards.cz: Pozveme Sally?

cards.en: Why shouldn't he know about it?
cards.cz: Proč by o tom neměl vědět?

cards.en: Why didn't you apply for the job? I'm sure you could have got it.
cards.cz: Proč jsi nepožádal o tu práci? Jsem si jist, že jsi ji mohl dostat.

cards.en: Why didn't you ask me? I could have lent you the money.
cards.cz: Proč ses mě nezeptal? Mohl jsem ti ty peníze půjčit.

cards.en: Why won't that child stop crying?
cards.cz: Proč to dítě nepřestane brečet?

cards.en: Why won't it work?
cards.cz: Proč to nechce fungovat?

cards.en: We weren't allowed to use dictionaries in the exam.
cards.cz: Při zkoušce jsme nesměli používat slovník.

cards.en: I'd lend you the record if I could find it.
cards.cz: Půjčil bych ti tu desku (gramofonovou), kdybych ji mohl najít.

cards.en: Shall we go out for dinner/supper this evening?
cards.cz: Půjdeme dnes večer ven na večeři?

cards.en: I'd better give you my phone number. You might need to contact me quickly.
cards.cz: Raději bych vám měl dát své telefonní číslo. Možná, že se se mnou budete potřebovat rychle spojit.

cards.en: We'd better let them know as soon as possible.
cards.cz: Raději bychom jim měli dát vědět co nejdříve.

cards.en: We'd better leave now, or we'll be late.
cards.cz: Raději bychom měli odejít teď, nebo přijdeme pozdě.

cards.en: We'd better not sit here (for) long. The sun's very strong today.
cards.cz: Raději bychom tady neměli sedět dlouho. Slunce je dnes velmi silné.

cards.en: You'd better not provoke him. He's in a terrible mood today.
cards.cz: Raději bys ho neměl provokovat. Má dnes strašnou náladu.

cards.en: You'd better not tell him. He won't like it at all.
cards.cz: Raději bys mu to neměl říkat. Vůbec se mu to nebude líbit.

cards.en: You'd better not phone them now. Perhaps they're/They might be having (their) lunch.
cards.cz: Radši bys jim teď neměl volat. Asi obědvají.

cards.en: Robin? Can you hear me?
cards.cz: Robině? Slyšíš mě?

cards.en: Marta's parents wouldn't let her go to the disco.
cards.cz: Rodiče nechtěli pustit Martu na diskotéku, [rodiče Marty]

cards.en: He told her he couldn't live without her.
cards.cz: Řekl jí, že bez ní nemůže žít.

cards.en: I told him I couldn't see anything.
cards.cz: Řekl jsem mu, že nic nevidím.

cards.en: She said she couldn't believe it.
cards.cz: Řekla, že tomu nemůže uvěřit.

cards.en: They said they couldn't go with us.
cards.cz: Řekli, že s námi nemohou jít.

cards.en: He was driving terribly fast. He could have caused an accident.
cards.cz: Řídil strašně rychle. Mohl způsobit nehodu.

cards.en: She says she won't be able to wait/she's not going to be able to wait for us.
cards.cz: Říká, že na nás nebude moct počkat, [ona]

cards.en: Has he always been allowed to speak like that to his parents/to speak to his parents like that?
cards.cz: Směl vždycky takhle mluvit k rodičům?

cards.en: She's allowed to go out only once a week.
cards.cz: Smí chodit ven jen jednou za týden, [ona]

cards.en: You're allowed to bring two friends with you.
cards.cz: Smíš si s sebou přivést dva kamarády.

cards.en: You're allowed to take three weeks of holiday/three weeks' holiday.
cards.cz: Smíš si vzít tři týdny dovolené.

cards.en: She tried, but she couldn't forget.
cards.cz: Snažila se, ale nemohla zapomenout.

cards.en: Whisper, or you might/may wake him (up).
cards.cz: Šeptej, jinak [nebo] bys ho mohl vzbudit.

cards.en: The box was so heavy (that) he couldn't even lift it.
cards.cz: Ta krabice byla tak těžká, že ji ani nezvedl.

cards.en: The box was heavy and wouldn't move.
cards.cz: Ta krabice byla těžká a nedalo se s ní hnout.

cards.en: My Dad's buying a new car. Unfortunately I won't be allowed to borrow/I'm not going to be allowed to borrow it.
cards.cz: Tatínek kupuje nové auto. Bohužel si ho nebudu smět půjčovat.

cards.en: The film can't have been very good. He fell asleep in the cinema.
cards.cz: Ten film nemohl být moc dobrý. On v kině usnul.

cards.en: The dog's not allowed to sit/The dog can't sit/The dog mustn't sit on the furniture.
cards.cz: Ten pes nesmí sedět na nábytku.

cards.en: The party can't/couldn't have been very good. He was home again by eight o'clock.
cards.cz: Ten večírek nemohl být moc dobrý. Do osmi hodin byl zase doma.

cards.en: I mustn't lose this letter.
cards.cz: Tenhle dopis nesmím ztratit.

cards.en: That was a long journey. You must be tired.
cards.cz: To byla dlouhá cesta. Musíte být unaveni.

cards.en: What a shame! You must have been disappointed.
cards.cz: To je ale škoda! Ty jsi musel být zklamaný.

cards.en: What beautiful shoes! They must have cost you a lot of money.
cards.cz: To jsou ale krásné boty! Musely tě stát hodně peněz.

cards.en: You're not allowed to do/You can't do/You mustn't do that.
cards.cz: To nesmíš dělat. .

cards.en: That should have occurred to you./You should have thought of that.
cards.cz: To tě mělo napadnout.

cards.en: I won't be able to translate/I'm not going to be able to translate this. It's very difficult.
cards.cz: Tohle nebudu moct přeložit. Je to velmi těžké.

cards.en: Petr can't have written this. This/It isn't his handwriting.
cards.cz: Tohle nemohl napsat Petr. To není jeho rukopis.

cards.en: This window won't open.
cards.cz: Tohle okno nejde otevřít.

cards.en: Tom fell asleep at/in the lecture. It must have been terribly boring.
cards.cz: Tom usnul na přednášce. Musela být strašně nudná.

cards.en: I have had to do that many times.
cards.cz: Toto jsem musel udělat mnohokrát.

cards.en: I'd do it if I could, but I can't.
cards.cz: Udělal bych to, kdybych mohl, ale nemohu.

cards.en: She's been able to read music since she was seven.
cards.cz: Umí číst noty od sedmi let [od té doby, co jí bylo sedm].

cards.en: Can he play a musical instrument?
cards.cz: Umí hrát na hudební nástroj? [on]

cards.en: He can play the guitar.
cards.cz: Umí hrát na kytaru, [on]

cards.en: Can she ride a bicycle?
cards.cz: Umí jezdit na kole? [ona]

cards.en: She can ride a horse.
cards.cz: Umí jezdit na koni, [ona]

cards.en: He can speak four languages.
cards.cz: Umí mluvit čtyřmi jazyky, [on]

cards.en: I can speak English.
cards.cz: Umím mluvit anglicky.

cards.en: I can use English modal verbs.
cards.cz: Umím používat anglická modální slovesa.

cards.en: He doesn't write to me any more. I must have said something he didn't like.
cards.cz: Už mi nepíše. Musela jsem říct něco, co se mu nelíbilo.

cards.en: You're not allowed to talk/You can't talk/You mustn't talk in the library.
cards.cz: V knihovně nesmíš mluvit.

cards.en: What time do you have to get up/have you got to get up in the morning?
cards.cz: V kolik musíš ráno vstát?

cards.en: What time do you have to/have you got to go/leave?
cards.cz: V kolik musíte odejít?

cards.en: We're allowed to go home early on Fridays.
cards.cz: V pátek smíme chodit domů dříve.

cards.en: I've never had to use my German in Prague.
cards.cz: V Praze jsem nikdy nemusela použít svou němčinu.

cards.en: I had to buy a new alarm clock yesterday.
cards.cz: Včera jsem musel koupit nový budík.

cards.en: I could have helped yesterday, but I'm afraid I haven't got (the) time today.
cards.cz: Včera jsem pomoct mohl, ale obávám se, že dnes nemám čas.

cards.en: We're not allowed to eat/We can't eat/We mustn't eat in class/in the classroom.
cards.cz: Ve třídě nesmíme jíst.

cards.en: He saw (that) she couldn't carry the suitcase and went to help her.
cards.cz: Viděl, že ten kufr nemůže unést, a šel jí pomoct.

cards.en: She looks terrible. She must be ill.
cards.cz: Vypadá hrozně. Musí být nemocná.

cards.en: It looks serious. We'd better call a/the doctor.
cards.cz: Vypadá to vážně. Raději bychom měli zavolat doktora.

cards.en: She explained to them why she couldn't visit them any more.
cards.cz: Vysvětlila jim, proč už je nemůže navštěvovat.

cards.en: You could always spend the night here.
cards.cz: Vždycky bys mohl strávit noc tady.

cards.en: She has always had to work very hard.
cards.cz: Vždycky musela velmi těžce pracovat.

cards.en: He's always been allowed to do what(ever) he wants/likes.
cards.cz: Vždycky směl dělat, co chce.

cards.en: He has always been able to sing well.
cards.cz: Vždycky uměl dobře zpívat.

cards.en: In a few months you'll be able to speak/you're going to be able to speak very good English.
cards.cz: Za pár měsíců budeš umět mluvit velmi dobrou angličtinou.

cards.en: He asked if he could come in.
cards.cz: Zeptal se, jestli může dál [dovnitř].

cards.en: She asked me if she could smoke in my office and I said she couldn't.
cards.cz: Zeptala se mě, jestli smí v mé kanceláři kouřit, a já řekl, že ne.

cards.en: I asked her again and again but she wouldn't tell me.
cards.cz: Znovu a znovu jsem se jí ptal, ale ona mi to nechtěla říct.

cards.en: Life here can't have been easy during the war.
cards.cz: Život tady za války nemohl být jednoduchý.

cards.en: They asked the teacher if/whether they were going to do present perfect soon.
cards.cz: Are we going to do present perfect soon? they asked the teacher.

cards.en: He asked them how they were going to repair it.
cards.cz: How are you going to repair it? he asked them.

cards.en: She asked me how many people I was going to invite.
cards.cz: How many people are you going to invite? she asked me.

cards.en: He asked her how much time she was going to need.
cards.cz: How much time are you going to need? he asked her.

cards.en: She asked the teacher if/whether the test was going to be difficult.
cards.cz: Is the test going to be difficult? she asked the teacher.

cards.en: She asked what they were going to do with it.
cards.cz: What are they going to do with it? she asked.

cards.en: She asked when it was going to happen.
cards.cz: When is it going to happen? she asked.

cards.en: He asked where the meeting was going to take place.
cards.cz: Where is the meeting going to take place? he asked.

cards.en: He asked who was going to explain it to her.
cards.cz: Who's going to explain it to her? he asked.

cards.en: She asked why they were going to build it there/here.
cards.cz: Why are they going to build it here? she asked.

cards.en: Is autumn coming?
cards.cz: Autumn is coming.

cards.en: There will be a lot of complaints, won't there?/There are going to be..., aren't there?
cards.cz: Bude mnoho stížností, že jo?

cards.en: It'll be better like that, won't it?
cards.cz: Bude to takhle lepší, že jo? [will/won't]

cards.en: You'll have to write to them more often, won't you?
cards.cz: Budeš jim muset psát častěji, že jo?

cards.en: You'll be able to do it yourself, won't you?
cards.cz: Budeš to moct udělat sám, že jo?

cards.en: I'll have to think about it, won't I?
cards.cz: Budu o tom muset přemýšlet, že ano?

cards.en: They live in Olomouc, don't they?
cards.cz: Bydlí v Olomouci, že jo? [oni]

cards.en: You've been to Greece three times, haven't you?
cards.cz: Byl jsi v Řecku již třikrát, že jo?

cards.en: He was surprised, wasn't he?
cards.cz: Byl překvapený, že jo?

cards.en: There was a good atmosphere (there), wasn't there?
cards.cz: Byla (tam) dobrá atmosféra, že jo?

cards.en: You were there, weren't you?
cards.cz: Byla jsi tam, že jo?

cards.en: They'd prefer it if I didn't go, wouldn't they?/They'd rather I didn't go, would they?
cards.cz: Byli by radši, kdybych nešel, že jo?

cards.en: They were pleased/glad, weren't they?
cards.cz: Byli rádi, že jo?

cards.en: It'd be better if we waited here, wouldn't it?
cards.cz: Bylo by lepší, kdybychom počkali tady, že jo?

cards.en: They're waiting for us, aren't they?
cards.cz: Cekají na nás, že jo?

cards.en: How much does he know about it?
cards.cz: Co [Kolik] o tom ví? [on]

cards.en: What are you looking for?
cards.cz: Co hledáš?

cards.en: a. What does he want? b. I need to know what he wants, c. Can you tell me what he wants?
cards.cz: Co chce? [on]

cards.en: a. What did you buy? b. I need to know what you bought, c. Can you tell me what you bought?
cards.cz: Co jste koupil?

cards.en: a. What did she send him? b. I need to know what she sent him. c. Can you tell me what she sent him?
cards.cz: Co mu poslala?

cards.en: a. What did they suggest? b. I need to know what they suggested, c. Can you tell me what they suggested?
cards.cz: Co navrhli?

cards.en: What is she listening to?
cards.cz: Co poslouchá? [ona]

cards.en: Can you tell me what he said?
cards.cz: Můžete mi říct co řekl?

cards.en: a. What do you think? b. I need to know what you think, c. Can you tell me what you think?
cards.cz: Co si myslíš?

cards.en: Can you tell me what it means?
cards.cz: Můžete mi říct co to znamená?

cards.en: a. What did they do? b. I need to know what they did. c. Can you tell me what they did?
cards.cz: Co udělali?

cards.en: What are you laughing at?
cards.cz: Čemu se směješ?

cards.en: Whose poems do you prefer?
cards.cz: Čí básně máš raději?

cards.en: Whose is this book?/Whose book is this?
cards.cz: Čí je tato kniha?

cards.en: Whose is this green jacket?/Whose green jacket is this?
cards.cz: Čí je tato zelená bunda?

cards.en: Whose jacket is it?
cards.cz: Čí je to sako?

cards.en: Whose are these shoes?/Whose shoes are these?
cards.cz: Čí jsou tyto boty?

cards.en: Whose car are we going in?
cards.cz: Čím [Koho] autem pojedeme?

cards.en: He gave you his address, didn't he?
cards.cz: Dal vám svou adresu, že ano?

cards.en: There aren't enough hours in a/the day, are there?
cards.cz: Den nemá [ve dni není] dost hodin, že ne?

cards.en: It's hot today, isn't it?
cards.cz: Dnes je horko, že jo?

cards.en: She wasn't feeling very well this morning, was she?
cards.cz: Dnes ráno se necítila moc dobře, že ne?

cards.en: They're not at home this evening, are they?
cards.cz: Dnes večer nejsou doma, že ne?

cards.en: He's working this evening, isn't he?
cards.cz: Dnes večer pracuje, že jo? [on]

cards.en: a. Who is he in love with? b. I need to know who he is in love with. c. Can you tell me who he is in love with?
cards.cz: Do koho je zamilovaný?

cards.en: There are thirty days in April, aren't there?
cards.cz: Duben má [V dubnu je] třicet dní, že jo?

cards.en: Is Emma going to be there?
cards.cz: Emma is going to be there.

cards.en: Is English difficult?
cards.cz: English is difficult.

cards.en: Did everyone come?
cards.cz: Everyone came.

cards.en: Did everyone dance?
cards.cz: Everyone danced.

cards.en: There's a film about his life, isn't there?
cards.cz: Existuje [Je] film o jeho životě, že jo?

cards.en: Can he read Chinese?
cards.cz: He can read Chinese.

cards.en: Can he surf?
cards.cz: He can surf. [Can...?]

cards.en: Does he dislike alcohol?
cards.cz: He dislikes alcohol.

cards.en: Did he drink a lot of wine?
cards.cz: He drank a lot of wine.

cards.en: Does he eat healthy food?
cards.cz: He eats healthy food.

cards.en: Does he exercise every morning?
cards.cz: He exercises every morning.

cards.en: Did he have a good idea?
cards.cz: He had a good idea.

cards.en: Did he have a headache?
cards.cz: He had a headache.

cards.en: Has he been looking for us?
cards.cz: He has been looking for us.

cards.en: Has he been to Australia?
cards.cz: He has been to Australia.

cards.en: Has he finished reading my book?
cards.cz: He has finished reading my book.

cards.en: Has he had enough?
cards.cz: He has had enough.

cards.en: Has he lost his passport?
cards.cz: He has lost his passport.

cards.en: Does he have to work/Must he work hard for the exams?
cards.cz: He has to/must work hard for the exams.

cards.en: Did he invite a lot of friends?
cards.cz: He invited a lot of friends.

cards.en: Is he eighteen?
cards.cz: He is eighteen.

cards.en: Is he looking at us?
cards.cz: He is looking at us.

cards.en: Does he prefer mineral water?
cards.cz: He prefers mineral water.

cards.en: Does he study English a lot?
cards.cz: He studies English a lot.

cards.en: What does he want to tell me?
cards.cz: He wants to tell me something.

cards.en: Was he shocked?
cards.cz: He was shocked.

cards.en: Was he sitting alone?
cards.cz: He was sitting alone.

cards.en: Was he walking along the street when it happened?
cards.cz: He was walking along the street when it happened.

cards.en: Where is he going this weekend?
cards.cz: He's going somewhere this weekend.

cards.en: When is he going to arrive?
cards.cz: He's going to arrive sometime.

cards.en: Where is he hiding?
cards.cz: He's hiding somewhere.

cards.en: Has her brother got a girlfriend? Does her brother have a girlfriend?
cards.cz: Her brother has got a girlfriend. Her brother has a girlfriend.

cards.en: Has his girlfriend got a cat? Does his girlfriend have a cat?
cards.cz: His girlfriend has got a cat. His girlfriend has a cat.

cards.en: You wanted to go/come with us, didn't you?
cards.cz: Chtěli jste jít s námi, že jo?

cards.en: Do I have to help/Must I help you with it?
cards.cz: I have to/must help you with it.

cards.en: How did it break?
cards.cz: It broke somehow.

cards.en: Who did it fall on?
cards.cz: It fell on someone, [who]

cards.en: Has it been snowing?
cards.cz: It has been snowing.

cards.en: Has it stopped raining?
cards.cz: It has stopped raining.

cards.en: Is it going to be a surprise?
cards.cz: It is going to be a surprise.

cards.en: Is it October?
cards.cz: It is October. [Is...?]

cards.en: Is it raining?
cards.cz: It is raining. [Is...?]

cards.en: Was it interesting?
cards.cz: It was interesting.

cards.en: Was it raining when they left the house?
cards.cz: It was raining when they left the house.

cards.en: Was it snowing on Saturday?
cards.cz: It was snowing on Saturday.

cards.en: Was it starting to get dark?
cards.cz: It was starting to get dark.

cards.en: Was it terrible?
cards.cz: It was terrible. [Was...?]

cards.en: Did Jack celebrate his birthday on Friday?
cards.cz: Jack celebrated his birthday on Friday.

cards.en: Did Jack enjoy the party?
cards.cz: Jack enjoyed the party.

cards.en: a. How should I study? b. I need to know how I should study, c. Can you tell me how I should study?
cards.cz: Jak bych měl studovat?

cards.en: How often do you go to the dentist?
cards.cz: Jak často chodíš k zubaři?

cards.en: How far is it from London to Edinburgh?
cards.cz: Jak daleko je z Londýna do Edinburghu?

cards.en: How long do we have to/How long must we wait?
cards.cz: Jak dlouho musíme čekat?

cards.en: How long is the film?
cards.cz: Jak dlouhý je ten film?

cards.en: a. How far is it? b. I need to know how far it is. c. Can you tell me how far it is?
cards.cz: Jak je to daleko?

cards.en: How did the party go?
cards.cz: Jak probíhal [šel] večírek?

cards.en: How do you make an omelette?
cards.cz: Jak se dělá omeleta?

cards.en: What is your name?
cards.cz: Jak se jmenuješ?

cards.en: How is her husband?
cards.cz: Jak se má její muž?

cards.en: How is your sister?
cards.cz: Jak se má tvá sestra?

cards.en: a. How are his parents? b. I need to know how his parents are. c. Can you tell me how his parents are?
cards.cz: Jak se mají jeho rodiče?

cards.en: How do you spell 'Shakespeare'?,
cards.cz: Jak se píše "Shakespeare"?

cards.en: How difficult is Czech for foreigners?
cards.cz: Jak těžká je čeština pro cizince?

cards.en: How does she do/make it?
cards.cz: Jak to dělá? [ona]

cards.en: How do you know?
cards.cz: Jak to víš?

cards.en: What does her husband look like?
cards.cz: Jak vypadá její muž?

cards.en: What does your sister look like?
cards.cz: Jak vypadá tvoje sestra?

cards.en: How do I look in this green dress?
cards.cz: Jak vypadám v těchto zelených šatech?

cards.en: What is your sister like?
cards.cz: Jaká je tvoje sestra?

cards.en: How was the party?/What was the party like?
cards.cz: Jaký byl večírek?

cards.en: What is her husband like?
cards.cz: Jaký je její muž?

cards.en: His car isn't white, is it?
cards.cz: Jeho auto není bílé, že ne?

cards.en: Her office is on the ground floor, isn't it?
cards.cz: Její kancelář je v přízemí, že ano?

cards.en: We were only/just trying to help them, weren't we?
cards.cz: Jenom jsme se jim pokoušeli pomoct, že ano?

cards.en: You haven't phoned Ivan yet, have you?
cards.cz: Ještě jsi Ivanovi nezavolal, že ne?

cards.en: It's still raining, isn't it?
cards.cz: Ještě prší, že jo?

cards.en: I'm a good student, aren't I?
cards.cz: Jsem dobrý student, že jo?

cards.en: They're at school now, aren't they?
cards.cz: Jsou teď ve škole, že jo?

cards.en: You're tired again, aren't you?
cards.cz: Jste zase unavená, že ano?

cards.en: Where does the number seven tram go?/Where does tram number seven go?
cards.cz: Kam jezdí tramvaj číslo sedm?

cards.en: a. Where does he live? b. I need to know where he lives, c. Can you tell me where he lives?
cards.cz: Kde bydlí? [on]

cards.en: Where do they live?
cards.cz: Kde bydlí? [oni]

cards.en: Where is his office?
cards.cz: Kde je jeho kancelář?

cards.en: a. Where is the station? b. I need to know where the station is. c. Can you tell me where the station is?
cards.cz: Kde je nádraží?

cards.en: Where's the post office?
cards.cz: Kde je pošta?

cards.en: Where's the toilet, please?
cards.cz: Kde je záchod, prosím?

cards.en: a. Where can I buy some stamps? b. I need to know where I can buy some stamps, c. Can you tell me where I can buy some stamps?
cards.cz: Kde mohu koupit nějaké známky?

cards.en: a. Where do you work? b. I need to know where you work. c. Can you tell me where you work?
cards.cz: Kde pracuješ?

cards.en: a. Where does it start? b. I need to know where it starts, c. Can you tell me where it starts?
cards.cz: Kde to začíná? [použijte start]

cards.en: a. Who teaches him? b. I need to know who teaches him. c. Can you tell me who teaches him?
cards.cz: Kdo ho učí?

cards.en: a. Who is her best friend? b. I need to know who her best friend is. c. Can you tell me who her best friend is?
cards.cz: Kdo je její nejlepší přítel?

cards.en: a. Who is that girl? b. I need to know who that girl is. c. Can you tell me who that girl is?
cards.cz: Kdo je ta dívka?

cards.en: Who is that man?
cards.cz: Kdo je ten pán?

cards.en: Who(m) does she dislike?/Who(m) doesn't she like?
cards.cz: Kdo se jí nelíbí?

cards.en: Who was that/it?
cards.cz: Kdo to byl?

cards.en: Who can swim?
cards.cz: Kdo umí plavat?

cards.en: Who knows the answer?
cards.cz: Kdo zná odpověď?

cards.en: When do you go to bed?
cards.cz: Kdy chodíš do postele?

cards.en: a. When is the lecture? b. I need to know when the lecture is. c. Can you tell me when the lecture is?
cards.cz: Kdy je ta přednáška?

cards.en: When are the holidays?
cards.cz: Kdy jsou prázdniny?

cards.en: When is Christmas in England?
cards.cz: Kdy jsou v Anglii Vánoce?

cards.en: When is your birthday?
cards.cz: Kdy máš narozeniny?

cards.en: a. When do they have lunch? b. I need to know when they have lunch, c. Can you tell me when they have lunch?
cards.cz: Kdy obědvají [mají oběd]?

cards.en: a. When does her bus arrive? b. I need to know when her bus arrives, c. Can you tell me when her bus arrives?
cards.cz: Kdy přijede její autobus?

cards.en: a. When does the post office open? b. I need to know when the post office opens, c. Can you tell me when the post office opens?
cards.cz: Kdy se otevírá pošta?

cards.en: a. When does summer(time) begin? b. I need to know when summer(time) begins, c. Can you tell me when summer(time) begins?
cards.cz: Kdy začíná léto?

cards.en: If he used his bicycle he wouldn't spend so much on petrol, would he?
cards.cz: Kdyby používal své kolo, neutrácel by tolik za benzin, že ne?

cards.en: There was a castle here once, wasn't there?
cards.cz: Kdysi tady býval hrad, že jo?

cards.en: a. Who did you meet there? b. I need to know who you met there, c. Can you tell me who you met there?
cards.cz: Koho jsi tam potkal?

cards.en: a. Who did they invite? b. I need to know who they invited, c. Can you tell me who they invited?
cards.cz: Koho pozvali?

cards.en: a. Who did he ask? b. I need to know who he asked, c. Can you tell me who he asked?
cards.cz: Koho se zeptal?

cards.en: How much sugar do you need for this recipe?
cards.cz: Kolik cukru potřebuješ na tento recept?

cards.en: How many days can you spend with us?
cards.cz: Kolik dní s námi můžete strávit?

cards.en: How many hours do you train each/every day?
cards.cz: Kolik hodin každý den trénuješ?

cards.en: a. What is the time?/What time is it? b. I need to know what the time is./I need to know what time it is. c. Can you tell me what the time is?/Can you tell me what time it is?
cards.cz: Kolik je hodin?

cards.en: a. How old is she? b. I need to know how old she is. c. Can you tell me how old she is?
cards.cz: Kolik je jí let?

cards.en: How many days are there in November?
cards.cz: Kolik má listopad [je v listopadu] dní?

cards.en: How much free time does he have?
cards.cz: Kolik má volného času? [on]

cards.en: How much money have you got in the bank?
cards.cz: Kolik máš peněz v bance?

cards.en: How many beers does he drink each/every evening?
cards.cz: Kolik vypije každý večer piv? [on]

cards.en: How many British actors do you know?
cards.cz: Kolik znáš britských herců?

cards.en: What is the date?/What date is it?
cards.cz: Kolikátého je?

cards.en: Who(m) do you prefer?
cards.cz: Komu dáváš přednost?

cards.en: Who is he writing to?
cards.cz: Komu píše? [on]

cards.en: They bought it in Paris, didn't they?
cards.cz: Koupili to v Paříži, že jo?

cards.en: Which chapter is (the) most difficult?
cards.cz: Která kapitola je nejtěžší?

cards.en: Which/On which days do you go to school?
cards.cz: Které dny chodíš do školy?

cards.en: Which countries does he want to visit?
cards.cz: Které země chce navštívit? [on]

cards.en: Which American writers do you know?
cards.cz: Které znáš americké spisovatele?

cards.en: Which colour do you prefer?
cards.cz: Kterou barvu máš raději?

cards.en: Which glass do you want?
cards.cz: Kterou chceš sklenici?

cards.en: Which house is theirs?/Which is their house?
cards.cz: Který je jejich dům?

cards.en: Which coat is yours?/Which is your coat?
cards.cz: Který je tvůj kabát?

cards.en: What/Which languages do you speak?
cards.cz: Kterými mluvíš jazyky?

cards.en: She has flown many times, hasn't she?
cards.cz: Letěla již mnohokrát, že jo?

cards.en: She liked our present/gift, didn't she?
cards.cz: Líbil se jí náš dárek, že jo?

cards.en: They couldn't go on holiday last year, could they?/They weren't able to go on holiday last year, were they?
cards.cz: Loni nemohli jet na dovolenou, že ne?

cards.en: She's got long, brown hair, hasn't she? She has long, brown hair, doesn't she?
cards.cz: Má dlouhé, hnědé vlasy, že jo? [ona]

cards.en: She's hungry, isn't she?
cards.cz: Má hlad, že jo?[ona]

cards.en: He's got a swimming pool in the/his garden, hasn't he? He has a swimming pool in the/his garden, doesn't he?
cards.cz: Má na zahradě bazén, že jo? [on]

cards.en: He's always right, isn't he?
cards.cz: Má vždycky pravdu, že ano? [on]

cards.en: We've got (the) time, haven't we? We have (the) time, don't we?
cards.cz: Máme čas, že jo?

cards.en: You've got two brothers, haven't you? You have two brothers, don't you?
cards.cz: Máte dva bratry, že ano?

cards.en: You had the same problem, didn't you?
cards.cz: Měl jsi ten samý problém, že jo?

cards.en: He had some good ideas, didn't he?
cards.cz: Měl nějaké dobré nápady, že jo?

cards.en: She should be more patient, shouldn't she? (She ought to be.., oughtn't she?)
cards.cz: Měla by být trpělivější, že jo?

cards.en: She had a difficult childhood, didn't she?
cards.cz: Měla těžké dětství, že jo?

cards.en: We should warn them about it, shouldn't we? (We ought to warn.., oughtn't we?)
cards.cz: Měli bychom je před tím varovat, že jo?

cards.en: They had a lot of questions, didn't they?
cards.cz: Měli hodně otázek, že jo?

cards.en: We had a terrible journey, didn't we?
cards.cz: Měli jsme strašnou cestu, že jo?

cards.en: They're talking about James, aren't they?
cards.cz: Mluví o Jamesovi, že jo? [oni]

cards.en: You speak German, don't you?
cards.cz: Mluvíte německy, že ano?

cards.en: Could you tell me how much this costs/is?
cards.cz: Mohl byste mi říct, kolik tohle stojí?

cards.en: Could you tell me how many people there are in London?
cards.cz: Mohla byste mi říct, kolik je v Londýně lidí?

cards.en: My English isn't improving, is it?
cards.cz: Moje angličtina se nezlepšuje, že ne?

cards.en: He had to explain it to them three times, didn't he?
cards.cz: Musel jim to třikrát vysvětlit, že jo?

cards.en: We had to wait (for) two hours, didn't we?
cards.cz: Museli jsme dvě hodiny čekat, že jo?

cards.en: They had to call a/the doctor, didn't they?
cards.cz: Museli zavolat lékaře, že ano?

cards.en: They must be back by nine, mustn't they?/They have to be back by nine, don't they?/ They've got to be back by nine, haven't they?
cards.cz: Musí být zpátky do devíti, že jo? [oni] [tři způsoby překladu muset]

cards.en: I must ask her how she makes such good soups.
cards.cz: Musím se jí zeptat, jak dělá tak dobré polévky, [ona]

cards.en: We must answer all these questions, mustn't we?/We have to answer all these questions, don't we?/We've got to answer all these questions, haven't we?
cards.cz: Musíme odpovědět na všechny tyto otázky, že ano? [tři způsoby překladu muset]

cards.en: Can you tell me where she lives?
cards.cz: Můžeš mi říct, kde bydlí? [ona]

cards.en: You can come at seven, can't you?
cards.cz: Můžeš přijít v sedm, že jo?

cards.en: Can you tell me when the next train leaves?
cards.cz: Můžete mi říct, kdy odjíždí příští vlak?

cards.en: Can you show me how you did it?
cards.cz: Můžete mi ukázat, jak jste to udělal?

cards.en: What are you waiting for?
cards.cz: Na co čekáš?

cards.en: What is this button for?
cards.cz: Na co je tohle tlačítko?

cards.en: What are you looking forward to?
cards.cz: Na co se těšíš?

cards.en: What are you sitting on?
cards.cz: Na čem sedíš?

cards.en: What/Which films does she go to?
cards.cz: Na jaké filmy chodí? [ona]

cards.en: Who are they waving at/to?
cards.cz: Na koho mávají?

cards.en: Who are you thinking about?
cards.cz: Na koho myslíš? [O kom přemýšlíš?]

cards.en: Who is she smiling at?
cards.cz: Na koho se usmívá? [ona]

cards.en: There isn't a bookshop on the square, is there?
cards.cz: Na náměstí není knihkupectví, že ne?

cards.en: He was waiting for someone when you met him, wasn't he?
cards.cz: Na někoho čekal, když jsi ho potkal, že jo?

cards.en: There were a lot of guests at the wedding, weren't there?
cards.cz: Na svatbě bylo mnoho hostů, že jo?

cards.en: There isn't enough time for everything, is there?
cards.cz: Na všechno není dost času, že ne?

cards.en: There won't be anything to say, will there?/There's not going to be..., is there?
cards.cz: Nebude co říct, že ne?

cards.en: He's not going to be very happy, is he?
cards.cz: Nebude moc šťastný, že ne? [(not) going to]

cards.en: There won't be much time for it, will there?/There's not going to be..., is there?
cards.cz: Nebude na to moc času, že ne?

cards.en: He won't be able to contact us, will he?
cards.cz: Nebude se s námi moct spojit, že ne? [on]

cards.en: She won't be able to afford it, will she?
cards.cz: Nebude si to moct dovolit, že ne? [ona]

cards.en: It won't be easy, will it?
cards.cz: Nebude to jednoduché, že ne? [will/won't]

cards.en: He won't believe it, will he?
cards.cz: Nebude tomu věřit, že ne? [on] [will/won't]

cards.en: You won't be angry, will you?
cards.cz: Nebudeš se zlobit, že ne? [will/won't]

cards.en: You won't have to do the exam again, will you?
cards.cz: Nebudeš tu zkoušku muset dělat znovu, že ne?

cards.en: There wasn't any toilet paper, was there?
cards.cz: Nebyl (tam) toaletní papír, že ne?

cards.en: You weren't at the party, were you?
cards.cz: Nebyl jsi na tom večírku, že ne?

cards.en: We weren't with him, were we?
cards.cz: Nebyli jsme s ním, že ne?

cards.en: There weren't enough places/seats, were there?
cards.cz: Nebylo (tam) dost míst, že ne?

cards.en: It wouldn't be so bad if they helped us occasionally, would it?
cards.cz: Nebylo by to tak špatné, kdyby nám příležitostně pomohli, že ne?

cards.en: There weren't many problems with it, were there?
cards.cz: Nebylo s tím mnoho problémů, že ne?

cards.en: It wasn't difficult/hard, was it?
cards.cz: Nebylo to těžké, že ne?

cards.en: I wouldn't make so many mistakes if I were less nervous, would I?
cards.cz: Nedělal bych tolik chyb, kdybych byl méně nervózní, že ne?

cards.en: She wouldn't do it if she didn't want to, would she?
cards.cz: Nedělala by to, kdyby nechtěla, že ne?

cards.en: They weren't looking at us, were they?
cards.cz: Nedívali se na nás, že ne?

cards.en: It doesn't work, does it?
cards.cz: Nefunguje to, že ne?

cards.en: I don't understand why she drinks so much.
cards.cz: Nechápu [nerozumím], proč tolik pije, [ona]

cards.en: She's not going out with your brother, is she?
cards.cz: Nechodí s tvým bratrem, že ne? [ona]

cards.en: He wouldn't go/come to the meetings if he weren't interested, would he?
cards.cz: Nechodil by na ty schůzky, kdyby neměl zájem, že ne?

cards.en: You're not going out today, are you?
cards.cz: Nejdeš dnes ven, že ne?

cards.en: I'm not sure how much milk there is in the fridge.
cards.cz: Nejsem si jist, kolik je v lednici mléka.

cards.en: I'm not fat, am I?
cards.cz: Nejsem tlustý, že ne?

cards.en: You're not sure/certain, are you?
cards.cz: Nejsi si jist, že ne?

cards.en: We're not satisfied, are we?
cards.cz: Nejsme spokojeny, že ne?

cards.en: There aren't any reasons for it, are there?
cards.cz: Nejsou pro to žádné důvody, že ne?

cards.en: You're not going to buy it, are you?
cards.cz: Nekoupíš to, že ne? [(not) going to]

cards.en: You don't like garlic, do you?
cards.cz: Nemáš rád česnek, že ne?

cards.en: He didn't have (the) time, did he?
cards.cz: Neměl čas, že ne?

cards.en: You didn't have his telephone number, did you?
cards.cz: Neměl jsi jeho telefonní číslo, že ne?

cards.en: She didn't have a chance, did she?
cards.cz: Neměla šanci, že ne?

cards.en: They shouldn't say such things, should they? (They ought not to say.., ought they?)
cards.cz: Neměli by říkat takové věci, že ne?

cards.en: We didn't have enough money, did we?
cards.cz: Neměli jsme dost peněz, že ne?

cards.en: We didn't have a bad holiday, did we?
cards.cz: Neměli jsme špatnou dovolenou, že ne?

cards.en: You couldn't believe it, could you?
cards.cz: Nemohl jste tomu uvěřit, že ne?

cards.en: We couldn't help him, could we?/We weren't able to help him, were we?
cards.cz: Nemohli jsme mu pomoct, že ne?

cards.en: They can't find him, can they?
cards.cz: Nemohou ho najít, že ne?

cards.en: You didn't have to pay for it yourself, did you? (You needn't have paid for it yourself, need you?)
cards.cz: Nemusel jsi za to platit sám, že ne?

cards.en: She didn't have to say it like that, did she? (She needn't have said it like that, need she?)
cards.cz: Nemusela to takhle říkat, že ne?

cards.en: She doesn't have to cook anything for us, does she?/She hasn't got to cook anything for us, has she?/She needn't cook anything for us, need she?
cards.cz: Nemusí pro nás nic vařit, že ne? [ona] [tři způsoby překladu muset]

cards.en: I don't have to sign it, do I?/I haven't got to sign it, have I?/I needn't sign it, need I?
cards.cz: Nemusím to podepsat, že ne? [tři způsoby překladu muset]

cards.en: You don't have to go yet, do you?/You haven't got to go yet, have you?/You needn't go yet, need you?
cards.cz: Nemusíš ještě jít, že ne? [tři způsoby překladu muset]

cards.en: There isn't any truth in it, is there?
cards.cz: Není na tom ani zbla pravdy [žádná pravda], že ne?

cards.en: It's not important, is it?
cards.cz: Není to důležité, že ne?

cards.en: It's not easy, is it?
cards.cz: Není to jednoduché, že ne?

cards.en: He's not in your class, is he?
cards.cz: Není ve tvé třídě, že ne? [on]

cards.en: They weren't bored, were they?
cards.cz: Nenudili se, že ne?

cards.en: He doesn't drink alcohol, does he?
cards.cz: Nepije alkohol, že ne? [on]

cards.en: You didn't send it express, did you?
cards.cz: Neposlal jsi to expres, že ne?

cards.en: You're not sitting on my keys, are you?
cards.cz: Nesedíš na mých klíčích, že ne?

cards.en: She wasn't allowed to go with us, was she?
cards.cz: Nesměla jít s námi, že ne?

cards.en: We weren't allowed to speak at the table, were we?
cards.cz: Nesměli jsme mluvit u stolu, že ne?

cards.en: He didn't agree with us, did he?
cards.cz: Nesouhlasil s námi, že ne?

cards.en: We're not looking forward to it, are we?
cards.cz: Netěšíme se na to, že ne?

cards.en: She can't ski, can she?
cards.cz: Neumí lyžovat, že ne? [ona]

cards.en: They didn't mind (it), did they?
cards.cz: Nevadilo jim to, že ne?

cards.en: It didn't matter, did it?
cards.cz: Nevadilo to, že ne?

cards.en: They don't know about it, do they?
cards.cz: Nevědí o tom, že ne?

cards.en: She doesn't believe it, does she?
cards.cz: Nevěří tomu, že ne? [ona]

cards.en: He doesn't know when the party is.
cards.cz: Neví, kdy je ten večírek.

cards.en: I don't know why they dislike/why they don't like him.
cards.cz: Nevím, proč se jim nelíbí, [on]

cards.en: She wasn't angry/cross, was she?
cards.cz: Nezlobila se, že ne?

cards.en: We didn't stay (for) long, did we?
cards.cz: Nezůstali jsme dlouho, že ne?

cards.en: We've never tried that/it, have we?
cards.cz: Nikdy jsme to nezkusili, že ne?

cards.en: They've never had much/a lot of money, have they?
cards.cz: Nikdy neměli mnoho peněz, že ne?

cards.en: You won't tell anyone, will you?
cards.cz: Nikomu to neřekneš, že ne? [will/won't]

cards.en: What was the lecture about?
cards.cz: O čem byla ta přednáška?

cards.en: a. What is the article about? b. I need to know what the article is about, c. Can you tell me what the article is about?
cards.cz: O čem je ten článek?

cards.en: What were they talking/speaking about?
cards.cz: O čem mluvili?

cards.en: Whose children is she looking after today?
cards.cz: O čí děti se dnes stará?

cards.en: Who are you reading about?
cards.cz: O kom čteš?

cards.en: It's going to rain at the weekend, isn't it?
cards.cz: O víkendu bude pršet, že jo? [(not) going to]

cards.en: She was in Prague at the weekend, wasn't she?
cards.cz: O víkendu byla v Praze, že jo?

cards.en: Who is the letter from?
cards.cz: Od koho je ten dopis?

cards.en: How do you know my brother?
cards.cz: Odkud [Jak] znáš mého bratra?

cards.en: Where is your friend from?/Where does your friend come from?
cards.cz: Odkud je tvůj přítel?

cards.en: Where does the bus leave/go from?
cards.cz: Odkud jede ten autobus?

cards.en: She's wearing his sweater/jumper, isn't she?
cards.cz: Ona má na sobě jeho svetr, že jo?

cards.en: She's not older than you, is she?
cards.cz: Ona není starší než ty, že ne?

cards.en: Spiders have got eight legs, haven't they? Spiders have eight legs, don't they?
cards.cz: Pavouci mají osm noh, že jo?

cards.en: They haven't written to us lately, have they?
cards.cz: Poslední dobou nám nenapsali, že ne?

cards.en: I need to know how it happened.
cards.cz: Potřebuji vědět, jak se to stalo.

cards.en: They're going to invite me too, aren't they?
cards.cz: Pozvou mě také, že jo? [(not) going to]

cards.en: She works with your husband, doesn't she?
cards.cz: Pracuje s tvým mužem, že jo? [ona]

cards.en: Who is the present for?
cards.cz: Pro koho je ten dárek?

cards.en: a. Who does she work for? b. I need to know who she works for. c. Can you tell me who she works for?
cards.cz: Pro koho pracuje? [ona]

cards.en: Who are you cooking it for?
cards.cz: Pro koho to vaříš?

cards.en: Why does he drive so fast?
cards.cz: Proč jede [řídí] tak rychle? [on]

cards.en: Why don't you exercise [tělocvikj/practise [na hudební nástroj]?
cards.cz: Proč necvičíš?

cards.en: Why do you listen to this music?
cards.cz: Proč posloucháš tuto hudbu?

cards.en: Why don't you ask her?
cards.cz: Proč se jí nezeptáš?

cards.en: Why do you think that?
cards.cz: Proč si to myslíš?

cards.en: Please ask him when he needs it by.
cards.cz: Prosím tě, zeptej se ho, dokdy to potřebuje.

cards.en: We'll have to repeat all this grammar next week, won't we?
cards.cz: Příští týden budeme muset opakovat celou tuto gramatiku, že ano?

cards.en: I'd like to hear how much he paid for it.
cards.cz: Rád bych slyšel, kolik za to zaplatil.

cards.en: I'd like to know why it always rains at the weekend.
cards.cz: Rád bych věděl, proč vždycky o víkendu prší.

cards.en: We understand (it), don't we?
cards.cz: Rozumíme tomu, že jo?

cards.en: I'd tell him if I knew, wouldn't I?
cards.cz: Řekl bych mu to, kdybych to věděl, že jo?

cards.en: I told you about it, didn't I?
cards.cz: Řekl jsem ti o tom, že jo?

cards.en: Tell me when we should leave.
cards.cz: Řekni mi, kdy bychom měli odejít.

cards.en: Who is she going out with?
cards.cz: S kým chodí? [ona]

cards.en: Who are you going to Italy with?
cards.cz: S kým jedeš do Itálie?

cards.en: a. Who did she go with? b. I need to know who she went with. c. Can you tell me who she went with?
cards.cz: S kým šla?

cards.en: Does she buy a lot of cakes?
cards.cz: She buys a lot of cakes.

cards.en: Can she explain grammar well?
cards.cz: She can explain grammar well.

cards.en: Can she make good chocolate cake?
cards.cz: She can make good chocolate cake.

cards.en: Does she drink a lot of whiskey?
cards.cz: She drinks a lot of whiskey.

cards.en: Does she get up at ten?
cards.cz: She gets up at ten. [Does...?]

cards.en: Does she go to bed at nine?
cards.cz: She goes to bed at nine.

cards.en: Did she have an exam yesterday?
cards.cz: She had an exam yesterday.

cards.en: Has she been borrowing money from you?
cards.cz: She has been borrowing money from you.

cards.en: Has she been thinking about him a lot?
cards.cz: She has been thinking about him a lot.

cards.en: Has she forgotten his number?
cards.cz: She has forgotten his number.

cards.en: Has she gone out?
cards.cz: She has gone out.

cards.en: Has she got a brother? Does she have a brother?
cards.cz: She has got a brother. She has a brother.

cards.en: Has she got a dog too? Does she have a dog too?
cards.cz: She has got a dog too. She has a dog too.

cards.en: Has she met his parents?
cards.cz: She has met his parents.

cards.en: Does she have to pay/Must she pay them today?
cards.cz: She has to/must pay them today.

cards.en: Is she feeling better?
cards.cz: She is feeling better.

cards.en: Is she going to look for a new job?
cards.cz: She is going to look for a new job.

cards.en: Who is she thinking about?
cards.cz: She is thinking about someone, [who]

cards.en: Is she tired?
cards.cz: She is tired.

cards.en: Who does she love?
cards.cz: She loves someone, [použijte who]

cards.en: What did she say?
cards.cz: She said something.

cards.en: Was she angry?
cards.cz: She was angry.

cards.en: Was she looking for us?
cards.cz: She was looking for us.

cards.en: Was she sleeping when he phoned?
cards.cz: She was sleeping when he phoned.

cards.en: Does she watch the television all day?
cards.cz: She watches the television all day.

cards.en: Will she like it?
cards.cz: She will like it.

cards.en: Where is she going tonight?
cards.cz: She's going somewhere tonight.

cards.en: Who is at the door?
cards.cz: Someone is at the door.

cards.en: Who is waiting outside?
cards.cz: Someone is waiting outside.

cards.en: Who stole the money?
cards.cz: Someone stole the money.

cards.en: Who told you about it?
cards.cz: Someone told you about it.

cards.en: What is happening?
cards.cz: Something is happening.

cards.en: You were sleeping when I phoned/called, weren't you?
cards.cz: Spal jsi, když jsem zavolal, že jo?

cards.en: Is summer ending?
cards.cz: Summer is ending.

cards.en: He shouldn't have said such a thing, should he? (He ought not to have said.., ought he?)
cards.cz: Takovou věc neměl říkat, že ne?

cards.en: The taxi driver asked us where we wanted to go.
cards.cz: Taxikář se nás zeptal, kam chceme jet.

cards.en: They'll be able to visit us more often now, won't they?
cards.cz: Teď nás budou moct navštěvovat častěji, že jo?

cards.en: It should be cooler this weekend, shouldn't it?
cards.cz: Tento víkend by mělo být chladněji, že jo?

cards.en: Are the birds singing?
cards.cz: The birds are singing.

cards.en: Has the cat got a problem? Does the cat have a problem?
cards.cz: The cat has got a problem. The cat has a problem.

cards.en: Are the leaves falling?
cards.cz: The leaves are falling.

cards.en: Did the neighbours complain?
cards.cz: The neighbours complained.

cards.en: Did the police come?
cards.cz: The police came.

cards.en: Is the sun shining?
cards.cz: The sun is shining.

cards.en: Is the wind blowing?
cards.cz: The wind is blowing.

cards.en: Does the work have to be/Must the work be finished tomorrow?
cards.cz: The work has to/must be finished tomorrow.

cards.en: Are there going to be a lot of guests?
cards.cz: There are going to be a lot of guests.

cards.en: Are they going to buy a new car?
cards.cz: They are going to buy a new car.

cards.en: Are they going to write to you?
cards.cz: They are going to write to you.

cards.en: Are they here?
cards.cz: They are here.

cards.en: Are they waiting for me?
cards.cz: They are waiting for me.

cards.en: Can they see us?
cards.cz: They can see us.

cards.en: What did they find there?
cards.cz: They found something there.

cards.en: Did they give him some nice presents?
cards.cz: They gave him some nice presents.

cards.en: Who did they give it to?
cards.cz: They gave it to someone, [who]

cards.en: Did they have a good time on Saturday?
cards.cz: They had a good time on Saturday.

cards.en: Did they have bad luck?
cards.cz: They had bad luck. [Did...?]

cards.en: Have they already left?
cards.cz: They have already left.

cards.en: Have they been looking forward to the party?
cards.cz: They have been looking forward to the party.

cards.en: Have they prepared the lunch?
cards.cz: They have prepared the lunch.

cards.en: Where in Prague do they live?/Where do they live in Prague?
cards.cz: They live somewhere in Prague.

cards.en: Did they play rock music?
cards.cz: They played rock music.

cards.en: Where do they want to travel (to) next year?
cards.cz: They want to travel somewhere next year.

cards.en: What were they looking for?
cards.cz: They were looking for something.

cards.en: Were they pleased?
cards.cz: They were pleased.

cards.en: Were they talking about her?
cards.cz: They were talking about her. [Were...?]

cards.en: Were they talking when the teacher came into the room?
cards.cz: They were talking when the teacher came into the room.

cards.en: You've often said that, haven't you?
cards.cz: To jsi říkal často, že jo?

cards.en: That hasn't happened for a long time, has it?
cards.cz: To už se dlouho nestalo, že ne?

cards.en: This is your bag, isn't it?
cards.cz: Toto je vaše taška, že ano?

cards.en: You're Fred's brother, aren't you?
cards.cz: Ty jsi Fredův bratr, že jo?

cards.en: You'd do it if you could, wouldn't you?
cards.cz: Udělal bys to, kdybys mohl, že jo?

cards.en: He can speak English, can't he?
cards.cz: Umí (mluvit) anglicky, že jo? [on]

cards.en: You can swim, can't you?
cards.cz: Umíš plavat, že jo?

cards.en: He's (already) finished writing his new book, hasn't he?
cards.cz: Už dopsal svou novou knihu, že jo?

cards.en: They won't have to come here any more, will they?
cards.cz: Už sem nebudou muset chodit, že ne?

cards.en: There's a telephone in the school building, isn't there?
cards.cz: V budově školy je telefon, že jo?

cards.en: Whose bag is the food in?
cards.cz: V čí tašce je to jídlo?

cards.en: There were several questions in his letter, weren't there?
cards.cz: V jeho dopise bylo několik otázek, že jo?

cards.en: There aren't any tomatoes in the fridge, are there?
cards.cz: V lednici nejsou rajčata, že ne?

cards.en: There are over (There are more than) seven million people in London, aren't there?
cards.cz: V Londýně žije [je] více než 7 milionů lidí, že jo?

cards.en: There are a lot of foreign students at our school, aren't there?
cards.cz: V naší škole je mnoho zahraničních studentů, že jo?

cards.en: There are twenty chapters in our textbook, aren't there?
cards.cz: V naší učebnici je dvacet kapitol, že jo?

cards.en: It was cold on Sunday, wasn't it?
cards.cz: V neděli byla zima, že jo?

cards.en: There was a storm in the night, wasn't there?
cards.cz: V noci byla bouřka, že jo?

cards.en: There wasn't anyone in the room, was there?
cards.cz: V té místnosti nikdo nebyl, že ne?

cards.en: There weren't many questions in the exam, were there?
cards.cz: V té zkoušce nebylo mnoho otázek, že ne?

cards.en: There's a mistake in this sentence, isn't there?
cards.cz: V této větě je chyba, že jo?

cards.en: There's a wine festival here in September, isn't there?
cards.cz: V září je tady festival vína, že jo?

cards.en: Your father's a bus driver, isn't he?
cards.cz: Váš otec je řidič autobusu, že ano?

cards.en: There will be another meeting on Thursday, won't there?/There's going to be..., isn't there?
cards.cz: Ve čtvrtek bude další schůze, že jo?

cards.en: There's a lot of industry in your town, isn't there?
cards.cz: Ve vašem městě je rozvinutý průmysl [hodně průmyslu], že jo?

cards.en: Did you see how many cigarettes he smoked yesterday evening/last night?
cards.cz: Viděl jsi, kolik včera večer vykouřil cigaret?

cards.en: Do you know how often he sees his parents?
cards.cz: Víš, jak často vídá své rodiče? [on]

cards.en: Do you know how Petr is?
cards.cz: Víš, jak se má Petr?

cards.en: Do you know when the Battle of Hastings was?
cards.cz: Víš, kdy byla bitva u Hastingsu?

cards.en: Do you know why he doesn't visit them any more?
cards.cz: Víš, proč je už nenavštěvuje? [on]

cards.en: Do you know why that/it is?
cards.cz: Víš, proč to (tak) je?

cards.en: Do you know where it is?
cards.cz: Víte, kde to je?

cards.en: You're not Mr Jackson, are you?
cards.cz: Vy nejste pan Jackson, že ne?

cards.en: It looks good, doesn't it?
cards.cz: Vypadá to dobře, že jo?

cards.en: He's always liked her, hasn't he?
cards.cz: Vždycky se mu líbila, že jo?

cards.en: When can we visit them?
cards.cz: We can visit them sometime.

cards.en: Do we have to change/Must we change trains?
cards.cz: We have to/must change trains.

cards.en: How will we do it?
cards.cz: We'll do it somehow.

cards.en: Were you having a shower when I phoned?
cards.cz: You were having a shower when I phoned.

cards.en: Will you be happy here?
cards.cz: You will be happy here. [Will...?]

cards.en: Will you have time for it?
cards.cz: You will have time for it.

cards.en: Will you help us?
cards.cz: You will help us.

cards.en: When will you tell me?
cards.cz: You'll tell me sometime.

cards.en: What is it made from/of/with?
cards.cz: Z čeho se to dělá?

cards.en: We'll be able to see the whole town from the top of the hill, won't we?
cards.cz: Z vrcholu toho kopce budeme moct vidět celé město, že jo?

cards.en: She asked us where the ticket office was.
cards.cz: Zeptala se nás, kde je předprodej lístků.

cards.en: Ask him where he found it.
cards.cz: Zeptej se ho, kde to našel.

cards.en: There won't be any grammar tomorrow, will there?/There's not going to be..., is there?
cards.cz: Zítra nebude žádná gramatika, že ne?

cards.en: Tomorrow's lesson is going to be interesting, isn't it?
cards.cz: Zítřejší hodina bude zajímavá, že jo? [(not) going to]

cards.en: He'd be angry if he heard about it, wouldn't he?
cards.cz: Zlobil by se, kdyby se o tom doslechl, že jo?

cards.en: There should have been at least someone from his family at the wedding.
cards.cz: Alespoň někdo z jeho rodiny měl být na té svatbě.

cards.en: Yes, there is a theatre in our town.
cards.cz: Ano, v našem městě je divadlo.

cards.en: There must be absolute silence during the written exam.
cards.cz: Během té písemné zkoušky musí být absolutní ticho.

cards.en: There's a post office near the square.
cards.cz: Blízko náměstí je pošta.

cards.en: There is soon to be a new edition of this dictionary.
cards.cz: Brzy bude (k mání) nové vydání tohoto slovníku.

cards.en: There is soon to be a new tax law.
cards.cz: Brzy bude nový daňový zákon.

cards.en: Be careful! There's a cat lying under the car.
cards.cz: Bud' opatrný! Pod autem leží kočka.

cards.en: Is there going to be a prize for the best photograph?
cards.cz: Bude cena za nejlepší fotografii?

cards.en: Is there going to be any homework today?
cards.cz: Bude dnes úkol?

cards.en: Is there going to be enough time?
cards.cz: Bude dost času?

cards.en: Are there going to be enough places/seats?
cards.cz: Bude dost míst?

cards.en: There are going to be a lot of complaints.
cards.cz: Bude hodně stížností.

cards.en: There are going to be/There will be a lot of them.
cards.cz: Bude jich hodně.

cards.en: There are going to be/There will be seven of us.
cards.cz: Bude nás sedm.

cards.en: There are going to be more tourists than last year.
cards.cz: Bude víc turistů než loni.

cards.en: Are there going to be any rich and famous people at the party?
cards.cz: Budou na tom večírku nějací bohatí a slavní lidé?

cards.en: There are going to be some surprises.
cards.cz: Budou nějaká překvapení.

cards.en: There are going to be some changes here.
cards.cz: Budou tady nějaké změny.

cards.en: Was there any reason for it?
cards.cz: Byl pro to nějaký důvod?

cards.en: Was there a queue?
cards.cz: Byla (tam) fronta?

cards.en: It would be quite a good textbook if there were more exercises in it.
cards.cz: Byla by to docela dobrá učebnice, kdyby v ní bylo více cvičení.

cards.en: There would be fewer road accidents if people didn't drive so fast.
cards.cz: Bylo by méně dopravních nehod, kdyby lidé nejezdili [neřídili] tak rychle.

cards.en: There would be a lot of complaints if we did that/it.
cards.cz: Bylo by mnoho stížností, kdybychom to udělali.

cards.en: There was enough for everyone.
cards.cz: Bylo dost pro každého.

cards.en: Was there enough snow for skiing?
cards.cz: Bylo dost sněhu na lyžování?

cards.en: There was a lot of work.
cards.cz: Bylo hodně práce.

cards.en: There were ten of them.
cards.cz: Bylo jich deset.

cards.en: There were five of us.
cards.cz: Bylo nás pět.

cards.en: There have been several films about him.
cards.cz: Bylo o něm několik filmů.

cards.en: Were there enough glasses for everyone?
cards.cz: Bylo pro každého dost sklenic?

cards.en: There was too much of it.
cards.cz: Bylo toho příliš mnoho.

cards.en: Were there many/a lot of people in the waiting room?
cards.cz: Bylo v čekárně hodně lidí?

cards.en: Were there any better offers?
cards.cz: Byly nějaké lepší nabídky?

cards.en: Were there any questions after the lecture?
cards.cz: Byly po přednášce nějaké otázky?

cards.en: Were there any problems with the translation?
cards.cz: Byly s tím překladem nějaké problémy?

cards.en: Have there been any changes here in the last/past five years?
cards.cz: Byly tady za posledních pět let nějaké změny?

cards.en: Did there use to be a castle in this town?
cards.cz: Býval v tomto městě hrad?

cards.en: I here used to be an Indian restaurant here.
cards.cz: Bývala tady indická restaurace.

cards.en: There used to be more of them.
cards.cz: Bývalo jich víc.

cards.en: There's a surprise waiting for you when you get home.
cards.cz: Ceká na tebe překvapení, až se dostaneš domů.

cards.en: What else was there in the parcel?
cards.cz: Co ještě [Co jiného] bylo v tom balíčku?

cards.en: What's happened? Has there been a fire?
cards.cz: Co se stalo? Hořelo? [Byl požár?]

cards.en: Isn't there going to be a break/an interval?
cards.cz: Copak nebude přestávka?

cards.en: Isn't there going to be a free day?
cards.cz: Copak nebude volný den?

cards.en: Aren't there any elephants at/in the zoo?
cards.cz: Copak v té zoo nejsou žádní sloni?

cards.en: Isn't there going to be a toilet on the bus?
cards.cz: Copak v tom autobuse nebude záchod?

cards.en: Wasn't there an interpreter?
cards.cz: Cožpak (tam) nebyl tlumočník?

cards.en: Wasn't there at least a shower?
cards.cz: Cožpak (tam) nebyla alespoň sprcha?

cards.en: Wasn't there an easier way/a simpler way?
cards.cz: Cožpak nebyl jednodušší způsob?

cards.en: Weren't there any other possibilities?
cards.cz: Cožpak nebyly žádné jiné možnosti?

cards.en: Weren't there any mistakes in my letter?
cards.cz: Cožpak v mém dopise nebyly chyby?

cards.en: Waiter, there's a fly in my beer!
cards.cz: Číšníku, v mém pivu je moucha!

cards.en: There are only twenty-four hours in a day.
cards.cz: Den má [Ve dni jsou] jenom dvacet čtyři hodiny.

cards.en: There can't be any post today. It's Sunday.
cards.cz: Dnes nemůže chodit [být] pošta. Je neděle.

cards.en: There isn't an English lesson today.
cards.cz: Dnes není hodina angličtiny.

cards.en: There aren't any/There are no stars in the sky tonight.
cards.cz: Dnes v noci nejsou na obloze žádné hvězdy.

cards.en: There's going to be a good play on the radio this evening.
cards.cz: Dnes večer bude v rádiu dobrá hra.

cards.en: There is a good film on the television this evening.
cards.cz: Dnes večer je v televizi dobrý film.

cards.en: There isn't a concert this evening.
cards.cz: Dnes večer není koncert.

cards.en: There isn't anything/There's nothing on (the) television this evening.
cards.cz: Dnes večer není nic v televizi.

cards.en: I hope there are going to be enough helpers.
cards.cz: Doufám, že bude dost pomocníků.

cards.en: I hope there's going to be enough money for the repairs.
cards.cz: Doufám, že na ty opravy bude dost peněz.

cards.en: I would understand if there were a reason for it.
cards.cz: Chápal bych, kdyby pro to byl důvod.

cards.en: There are enough of them.
cards.cz: Je jich dost.

cards.en: There are too many of them.
cards.cz: Je jich příliš mnoho.

cards.en: Is there a pharmacy/chemist's near here?
cards.cz: Je tady blízko lékárna?

cards.en: Is there a telephone here?
cards.cz: Je tady telefon?

cards.en: Is there an airport in Manchester?
cards.cz: Je v Manchesteru letiště?

cards.en: Is there a technical museum in Prague?
cards.cz: Je v Praze technické muzeum?

cards.en: Is there any sugar in the cupboard?
cards.cz: Je ve skříni cukr?

cards.en: Is there a copy of King Lear in the school library?
cards.cz: Je ve školní knihovně výtisk [kopie] Krále Leara?

cards.en: Isn't there a blackboard in the classroom?
cards.cz: Je ve třídě tabule?

cards.en: Is there a photocopier/a xerox machine in/at your office?
cards.cz: Je ve vaší kanceláři kopírka?

cards.en: There hasn't been (any) time for it yet.
cards.cz: Ještě na to nebyl čas.

cards.en: There haven't been any complaints yet.
cards.cz: Ještě nebyly žádné stížnosti.

cards.en: There are three of us.
